summary: Renamed many fighter translation units and headers from placeholder motion-state-style filenames to descriptive action filenames, then updated obj_files.mk and local include references accordingly. The largest affected groups were Donkey Kong cargo/heavy actions and Master Hand action files, with smaller cleanup for AppealS/Guard naming in CLink, Dr Mario, Fox, and Yoshi.
what_changed: Updated obj_files.mk TEXT_O_FILES entries so renamed fighter source files are built under their new descriptive paths.

Renamed Donkey Kong placeholder files such as ftDk_MS_341, ftDk_MS_342, ftDk_MS_345_1, ftDk_MS_346, ftDk_MS_347, ftDk_MS_348, and ftDk_MS_349 to HeavyWait0, HeavyWalk, HeavyTurn, HeavyWait1, HeavyFall, HeavyJump, and HeavyLanding respectively.

Renamed many Master Hand placeholder files from ftMh_MS_* names to action-oriented names including Wait1_0, Wait1_2, Entry, Damage_0, Sweep, SweepWait, Slap, Walk, Drill, RockCrush, PaperCrush, Poke, FingerBeam, FingerGun, FingerGun3, BackAirplane1/2/3, BackCrush_0/1, BackDisappear, Squeeze, Squeezing, Throw, Slam, TagCrush, TagApplaud, TagRockPaper, TagCancel, CaptureMasterHand, and ThrownMasterHand.
Renamed clone/special-case appeal files from generic Appeal to AppealS for CLink, DrMario, and Fox, and updated related includes including Falco's include of Fox appeal code.
Renamed Yoshi's ftYs_Shield to ftYs_Guard and updated obj_files.mk and include references.

Updated source files' self-includes and cross-includes to match the new header names, e.g. Donkey heavy-action files now include ftDk_HeavyWait0.h, ftDk_HeavyFall.h, ftDk_HeavyJump.h, and related descriptive headers.
Updated at least one source comment in ftDonkey/types.h from referencing ftDk_MS_342.c to ftDk_HeavyWalk.c.
smart_moves: The PR used a scripted rename_tu workflow shown in the PR body, which is a good way to keep source/header renames, include references, and build-file changes consistent during broad TU naming cleanup.
The renamed files preserve build ordering in obj_files.mk while swapping only the filenames, reducing risk of accidental link/order changes.
Headers were renamed alongside their matching .c files, and self-includes were updated immediately, avoiding stale filename drift.
Placeholder MS-number filenames were replaced with action names tied to fighter behavior, improving grepability and making future motion-state work easier to navigate.
The cleanup touched comments and dependent includes, not just filesystem paths, which helps keep later decomp notes from pointing to obsolete placeholder filenames.
decomp_lessons: When a motion-state placeholder filename has a known action role, prefer a descriptive fighter-action filename over an MS-number name, but keep numeric suffixes such as _0/_1 where they distinguish still-ambiguous variants.

Broad TU renames should update obj_files.mk, the .c self-include, the matching header filename, cross-includes in neighboring action files, and any comments or data references that mention the old TU name.
Descriptive filenames are useful decomp infrastructure: they make future function matching, motion-state table review, and fighter behavior searches easier even when no code logic changes.
Renaming should be treated as a build-sensitive operation in this repository because obj_files.mk explicitly lists source object paths.
A PR that only renames files can still materially improve decomp maintainability by replacing opaque names like ftMh_MS_367.c with semantic names like ftMh_BackAirplane1.c.
naming_conventions: Fighter files use the ft prefix plus fighter abbreviation and PascalCase action name, e.g. ftDk_HeavyFall.c, ftMh_FingerBeam.c, ftFx_AppealS.c.
Side taunt/appeal files were normalized to AppealS rather than generic Appeal for CLink, DrMario, and Fox.
Yoshi's shield-related file was named Guard, matching a broader fighter action terminology preference visible in ftYs_Guard.c.
Master Hand action files keep descriptive names and retain numeric suffixes for distinct variants, e.g. ftMh_BackCrush_0.c, ftMh_BackCrush_1.c, ftMh_Wait1_0.c, ftMh_Wait1_1.c, ftMh_Wait1_2.c.
Donkey Kong cargo/heavy action files use a Heavy* prefix for the group, e.g. HeavyWait0, HeavyWait1, HeavyWalk, HeavyTurn, HeavyFall, HeavyJump, HeavyLanding.
Matching headers should mirror the translation unit basename exactly, e.g. ftDk_HeavyJump.c includes ftDk_HeavyJump.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching changes are evident; the diff is dominated by file renames and include/build-reference updates.
The rename-only approach preserves source contents and object ordering, which is a low-risk tactic for improving organization without perturbing matching behavior.
Because obj_files.mk directly names object paths, matching-preserving renames must update those paths atomically with the file move.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename remaining fighter MS-number files to semantic action names.
Search for stale references to ftDk_MS_341, ftDk_MS_342, ftMh_MS_*, ftFx_Appeal, ftCl_Appeal, ftDr_Appeal, or ftYs_Shield after PR 821.
Check whether Donkey Kong ftDk_MS_345_0 remained intentionally unnamed after the Heavy* cleanup.
Review Master Hand motion-state tables in ftdata.c and ftdata3.c to confirm all renamed action files line up with their motion-state IDs.
